title: Build Tool Comparison: Maven vs Gradle

description.task: Create a BuildToolComparison class that models key differences between Maven and Gradle. Implement a method that returns characteristics (convention-based vs script-based, XML vs Groovy/Kotlin DSL, build lifecycle) as a structured map.
instructions: compareTools() must return entries for both 'maven' and 'gradle'
Each tool map must include keys: 'configFormat', 'buildApproach', 'performanceModel'
Maven configFormat should be 'XML (pom.xml)'; Gradle configFormat should be 'Groovy/Kotlin DSL (build.gradle)'
Maven buildApproach should be 'convention-based'; Gradle should be 'script-based'
recommendTool() returns 'gradle' when largeProjectWithCustomBuilds=true and preferXml=false; otherwise returns 'maven'
description.hint: Use Map.of() or put() to populate the inner maps. For recommendation logic, check both flags with && and ||.
description.expectedOutput:
Maven config format: XML (pom.xml)
Gradle config format: Groovy/Kotlin DSL (build.gradle)
Recommendation: gradle

description.task: Implement a BuildLifecycle class that simulates Maven's default build lifecycle. The class should execute phases in order (validate → compile → test → package → install → deploy) and support skipping individual phases.
instructions: PHASES list is fixed: validate, compile, test, package, install, deploy (in this order)
skipPhase() adds the given phase name to the skippedPhases set
runUpTo() iterates from the first phase to targetPhase (inclusive) in order
Phases in skippedPhases are not added to executedPhases or returned
If targetPhase is not in PHASES, return an empty list
description.hint: Use PHASES.indexOf(targetPhase) to find the cutoff index, then loop from 0 to that index (inclusive), checking skippedPhases for each phase.

description.task: Create a ProjectStructureValidator class that validates whether a given project layout follows Maven's standard directory structure. Check for the presence of key directories and report any missing ones.
instructions: validate() must check all 4 entries in REQUIRED_DIRS
isValid is true only when no required directories are missing
missingDirs contains exactly the REQUIRED_DIRS entries not found in projectDirs
The order of entries in missingDirs should match their order in REQUIRED_DIRS
The method must not modify the input set
description.hint: Loop through REQUIRED_DIRS and check if each entry is contained in projectDirs. Collect missing ones. Set isValid = missingDirs.isEmpty().
